name: Crowbar
effect: Using a crowbar grants advantage to Strength checks where the crowbar's leverage can be applied.
description: There are no locked doors, only doors that haven't met enough encouragement.

name: Hunting Trap
effect: When set, this saw-toothed steel trap snaps shut on creatures that trigger its pressure plate. Escaping requires a DC 13 Strength check.
description: Excellent for catching wolves, goblins, and overconfident adventurers.

name: Healer's Kit
effect: Contains 10 uses. As an action, expend 1 use to stabilize a creature at 0 hit points without a Medicine check.
description: For when your battle plan was 'don't get stabbed' and it didn't work out.

name: Climber's Kit
effect: Can be used to anchor yourself, preventing falls greater than 25 feet from the anchor point.

name: Grappling Hook
description: Step one: throw hook. Step two: pray. Step three: pretend you meant to do that.

name: Rope (Silk)
effect: Stronger and lighter than normal rope.
description: Every adventurer buys fifty feet of rope. The wise ones buy a second coil.

name: Signal Whistle
description: Ideal for emergencies, ambushes, and annoying everyone within half a mile.

name: Acid
effect: Thrown up to 20 feet or splashed on a creature within reach; a successful ranged attack deals 2d6 acid damage.
description: Handle the cork gently — it holds back more argument than it looks like it should.

name: Alchemist's Fire
effect: Hurled up to 20 feet, shattering into clinging flame; a hit deals 1d4 fire damage at the start of each of the target's turns until a DC 10 Dexterity check smothers it.
description: Sold with a complimentary lecture on why you should aim, not juggle.

name: Antitoxin
effect: A single vial grants advantage on saving throws against poison for 1 hour. Undead and constructs gain nothing from it.
description: Smells like a mistake and tastes like a worse one, but it works.

name: Ball Bearings
effect: Scattered to cover a 10-foot-square area; creatures crossing at full speed must succeed on a DC 10 Dexterity save or fall prone.
description: A pouchful of chaos, best deployed just before someone says 'charge.'

name: Caltrops
effect: Spread across a 5-foot-square area; creatures entering at full speed must succeed on a DC 15 Dexterity save or stop moving and take 1 piercing damage, with speed reduced by 10 feet until healed.
description: The floor's way of saying 'not one step further.'

name: Candle
effect: Burns for 1 hour, shedding bright light in a 5-foot radius and dim light for another 5 feet.
description: A small, stubborn flame that outlasts most arguments about who forgot the torches.

name: Oil
effect: Thrown as an improvised weapon to coat a creature, or poured to cover a 5-foot-square area; if ignited, deals 5 fire damage to those in the area, or an extra 5 fire damage to a coated target struck by fire within 1 minute.
description: One pint, endlessly useful: lamps, hinges, and the occasional very bad afternoon for a goblin.

name: Torch
effect: Burns for 1 hour, providing bright light in a 20-foot radius and dim light for another 20 feet. A melee hit with a lit torch deals 1 fire damage.
description: Every dungeon's most honest source of light, and its clumsiest weapon.

name: Lamp
effect: Casts bright light in a 15-foot radius and dim light for another 30 feet; burns for 6 hours on a pint of oil.
description: Steadier than a torch, and far less likely to set your sleeve on fire.

name: Manacles
effect: Restrains a Small or Medium creature. Escaping requires a DC 20 Dexterity check; breaking them requires a DC 20 Strength check. Comes with one key — without it, thieves' tools and a DC 15 Dexterity check can pick the lock. Has 15 hit points.
description: Comes with exactly one key and an implicit promise you'll keep track of it.

name: Magnifying Glass
effect: Grants advantage on ability checks made to appraise or inspect small or highly detailed objects. Can substitute for flint and steel to start a fire in direct sunlight, given tinder and about 5 minutes.
description: Turns fine print into large print, and a sunny afternoon into a campfire.
